name: Katana
description: Iconic single-edged curved sword used by samurai.

name: Tachi
description: Predecessor to the katana.

name: Chokuto
description: Early Japanese straight sword with a single edge.

name: Tsurugi
description: Double-edged straight sword from ancient Japan.

name: Wakizashi
description: Shorter companion sword to the katana.

name: Tanto
description: Japanese dagger or short sword designed primarily for stabbing and close-range defense.

name: Odachi
description: Massive Japanese greatsword used by infantry to tackle cavalry on the battlefield.

name: Gladius
description: Standard infantry sword of the Roman legions.

name: Xiphos
description: Double-edged.

name: Longsword
description: Versatile two-handed sword with a cruciform hilt.

name: Rapier
description: Slender.

name: Claymore
description: Large two-handed greatsword used by Scottish highlanders for powerful sweeping attacks.

name: Cutlass
description: Short.

name: Viking Sword
description: Evolution of the Roman spatha.

name: Falchion
description: Single-edged sword with a weighted tip.

name: Bastard Sword
description: Also known as a hand-and-a-half sword.

name: Zweihänder
description: Enormous two-handed sword used by Landsknechte to break pike formations.

name: Smallsword
description: Lightweight civilian sidearm with a triangular blade.

name: Jian
description: Elegant double-edged straight sword.

name: Dao
description: Single-edged Chinese saber used for slashing and chopping.

name: Liuyedao
description: Willow leaf saber used by both cavalry and infantry during the Ming and Qing dynasties.

name: Zhanmadao
description: Horse-chopping saber with a long blade and handle.

name: Dadao
description: Heavy.

name: Shamshir
description: Lion's tail saber with a radical curve.

name: Kilij
description: Distinctive Ottoman saber with a flared tip (yalman) for increased chopping power.

name: Scimitar
description: General term for curved Middle Eastern sabers.

name: Yatagan
description: Recurved blade without a guard.

name: Talwar
description: Iconic Indian saber with a disc pommel.

name: Khanda
description: Heavy.

name: Pata
description: Unique weapon where the blade is integrated into a protective steel gauntlet.

name: Kris
description: Asymmetrical dagger with a distinctive wavy blade.

name: Kampilan
description: Large single-edged sword with a dual-horned pommel.

name: Dha
description: Versatile single-edged sword used for both daily utility and warfare across Mainland Southeast Asia.

name: Khopesh
description: Ancient Egyptian sickle-sword developed from battle-axes.

name: Kaskara
description: Straight.

name: Shotel
description: S-shaped sword with a radical curve designed to reach around an opponent's shield.

name: Macuahuitl
description: Wooden club embedded with sharp obsidian blades.

name: Ulfberht
description: High-quality Viking swords made from crucible steel, often inscribed with the name \+VLFBERH+T\"."

name: Seax
description: A large knife or short sword used by Germanic peoples, characterized by a single edge and a straight back.

name: Estoc
description: A long, narrow sword designed specifically for piercing chainmail or gaps in plate armor.

name: Flamberge
description: A sword with a wave-edged blade, believed to cause vibrations when parrying and more severe wounds.

name: Katzbalger
description: A short Renaissance sword used by Landsknecht mercenaries, known for its distinctive S-shaped guard.

name: Schweizersabel
description: A Swiss saber that combined a curved blade with a complex hilt, often used by Swiss mercenaries.

name: Dusack
description: A simple, single-edged short sword or training weapon, often made of a single piece of metal with a cut-out handle.

name: Flyssa
description: A traditional sword of the Kabyle people of Algeria, featuring a long, needle-like point and intricate engraving.

name: Pulwar
description: An Afghan sword similar to the Persian shamshir, featuring a disc-shaped pommel and a curved blade.

name: Mambele
description: A curved throwing knife or sword from Central Africa, often with multiple projections and used for both combat and ritual.

name: Ikalakala
description: A heavy, short sword from the Congo Basin, typically a broad, flared tip and used as a status symbol.

name: Takouba
description: A traditional sword of the Tuareg and other Sahelian ethnic groups, featuring a straight blade and cross-shaped hilt.

name: Achuela
description: A small hatchet or machete-like blade used in Spanish-influenced regions for utility and boarding actions.

name: Ginunting
description: A short, forward-curved sword used by the Philippine Marines, known for its extreme effectiveness in close-quarters jungle warfare.

name: Barong
description: A heavy, single-edged leaf-shaped sword used by the Tausug people of the Sulu Archipelago.

name: Cinquedea
description: A wide-bladed civilian short sword from Renaissance Italy, named for the five-finger width of its blade.

name: Shashka
description: A guardless saber of Circassian origin, popularized by Russian Cossacks as an extremely fast cavalry weapon.

name: Messer
description: A large, single-edged knife-like sword used by the common people in the Holy Roman Empire.

name: Firangi
description: An Indian sword with a European-made blade, featuring a distinctive basket hilt.

name: Nimcha
description: A North African saber with a characteristic knuckle guard and a slight curve.

name: Balato
description: A traditional sword of the Nias people, known for its distinctive animal-shaped pommel.
